info.title: Transparency Platform Restful API
info.description:
Auto-generated from the official ENTSO-E Postman collection by
`scripts/postman_to_openapi.jl`.

The real ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API exposes a single endpoint that is
dispatched purely by query parameters. To make this spec useful for code
generation, each Postman request has been mapped to a SYNTHETIC path of the
form `/{group}/{request-name}`. A client implemented from this spec must
route every operation back to the real base URL with the query parameters
attached.

servers.description: ENTSO-E Transparency Platform (synthetic paths -- see info.description)

Response limit: A maximum of 100 TimeSeries elements is returned per XML response.

parameters.name: in_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Border (or Bidding Zone for Flow Based Allocations) - same as out_Domain

get.description:
Request limit: Each request may cover a period of up to 1 year.
Response limit: A maximum of 100 TimeSeries elements is returned per XML response. The `offset`parameter can be used to retrieve the data in batches of up to 100 TimeSeries, where `offset=0` returns the first 100 elements, `offset=100` returns the next 100, and so on.

parameters.name: DocumentType
parameters.description: A31 = Agreed capacity; B33 = Published offered capacity

parameters.name: Out_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Control Area, Bidding Zone or Bidding Zone Aggregation

parameters.name: In_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Control Area, Bidding Zone or Bidding Zone Aggregation

parameters.name: Update_DateAndOrTime
parameters.description:
For Offered Capacity Evolution can be quried with datetime in numeric. For example 20210803113900000 for evolution update date time 03.08.2021 13:39:00.000.

If there is no OC evolution version with requested update_DateAndOrTime the system selects OC version valid in the given time (e.g., closest previous update_DateAndOrTime). The most recent publish OC version is provided in case the update_DateAndOrTime parameter is omitted. The most recent published OC version and intermediate OC versions is distinguished by document type:
−	Intermediate OC values: A31
−	Most recent published OC values: B33

get.description:
Request limit: Each request may cover a period of up to 1 year.
Response limit: A maximum of 100 TimeSeries elements is returned per XML response. The `offset`parameter can be used to retrieve the data in batches of up to 100 TimeSeries, where `offset=0` returns the first 100 elements, `offset=100` returns the next 100, and so on.

parameters.name: out_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Bidding Zone

parameters.name: in_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Bidding Zone (must be same as out_Domain)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Integer: Zero‑based index of the first TimeSeries to return. The offset parameter paginates the response in batches of 100 TimeSeries (e.g., offset = n returns the TimeSeries in the range n+1 to n+100).

get.description: Request limit: Each request may cover a period of up to 1 year. - It applies to PeriodStart and PeriodEnd if PeriodStartUpdate and PeriodEndUpdate parameters are not included in the request. - It applies only to PeriodStartUpdate and PeriodEndUpdate if included in the request. Response limit: A maximum of 200 XML documents contained in the ZIP response. The `offset`parameter can be used to retrieve the data in batches of up to 200 XML documents, where `offset=0` returns the first 200 documents, `offset=200` returns the next 200, and so on.

parameters.name: BiddingZone_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Control Area, Bidding Zone (optional if mRID is present)

parameters.name: BusinessType
parameters.description: A53 = Planned maintenance; A54 = Forced unavailability (unplanned outage)

parameters.name: DocStatus
parameters.description: A05 = Active; A09 = Cancelled; A13 = Withdrawn; (when not defined only Active and Cancelled outages are returned)

parameters.name: mRID
parameters.description: Older versions of an outage is returned only when mRID parameter is used

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Integer: Zero‑based index of the first XML document to return. The offset parameter paginates the response in batches of 200 XML documents (e.g., offset = n returns the XMLs in the range n+1 to n+200).

get.description: Request limit: Each request may cover a period of up to 1 year. - It applies to PeriodStart and PeriodEnd if PeriodStartUpdate and PeriodEndUpdate parameters are not included in the request. - It applies only to PeriodStartUpdate and PeriodEndUpdate if included in the request. Response limit: A maximum of 200 XML documents contained in the ZIP response. The `offset`parameter can be used to retrieve the data in batches of up to 200 XML documents, where `offset=0` returns the first 200 documents, `offset=200` returns the next 200, and so on.

parameters.name: ControlArea_Domain
parameters.description: EIC code of a Scheduling Area

parameters.name: DocStatus
parameters.description: A05: Active; A09: Cancelled; A13: Withdrawn

parameters.name: PeriodStartUpdate
parameters.description: Pattern yyyyMMddHHmm e.g. 201601010000 (mandatory if PeriodStart and PeriodEnd are not defined)

parameters.name: PeriodEndUpdate
parameters.description: Pattern yyyyMMddHHmm e.g. 201601010000 (mandatory if PeriodStart and PeriodEnd are not defined)

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Integer: Zero‑based index of the first XML document to return. The offset parameter paginates the response in batches of 200 XML documents (e.g., offset = n returns the XMLs in the range n+1 to n+200).

parameters.name: mRID
parameters.description: If mRID is included, individual versions of the particular event are queried using the rest of the parameters
